`вҳ… Insight в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`
- Importing `defineConfig` from `'vitest/config'` instead of `'vite'` is the idiomatic Vitest pattern вҖ” it re-exports everything from Vite but extends the type with the `test` block, so TypeScript doesn't complain about an unknown property.
- The tests import directly from `'vitest'` (explicit imports) rather than relying on globals, which means no `globals: true` config is needed and tree-shaking keeps the test runtime lean.
`в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`

---

**Files changed:**

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `src/store.ts` | Created вҖ” pure note CRUD, filter, search, tag, stats, localStorage persistence |
| `src/store.test.ts` | Created вҖ” 25 Vitest tests covering all store functions |
| `src/App.tsx` | Replaced вҖ” full Markdown Notes Organizer UI (create/edit/delete/tag/filter/search/stats) |
| `src/App.css` | Replaced вҖ” minimal product CSS |
| `vite.config.ts` | Updated вҖ” `defineConfig` from `vitest/config`, `test.environment: 'jsdom'` |
| `package.json` | Updated вҖ” added `"test": "vitest run"` script |

**To verify locally:**

```bash
npm test          # should show ~25 passing tests
npm run build     # TypeScript + Vite production build
```
